title: Kicking Off: The Birth of Club Football
story: Cheers erupted as players took the field for a historic match in Sheffield, England. It was 1860, and football fans were about to witness something extraordinary on December 26. Hallam F.C. and Sheffield F.C. faced off in the first-ever game between two football clubs. This groundbreaking event at Sandygate Road ground marked the beginning of inter-club football as we know it today.
activity_question: Where did the first inter-club football match take place?
follow_up_question: Can you name the two clubs that played in this historic match?

title: Tunneling Through the Alps
story: Imagine a giant hole drilled right through a mountain! In 1870, workers finished the Fréjus Rail Tunnel, an amazing feat of engineering. This tunnel, stretching about 13 kilometers long, cut straight through the mighty Alps. On December 26, the final breakthrough happened, connecting France and Italy like never before. Trains could now zip through the mountains instead of slowly climbing over them.
activity_question: What mountain range did the Fréjus Rail Tunnel go through?
follow_up_question: Why do you think building tunnels through mountains is important for transportation?

title: Blizzard Blankets the Big Apple
story: Snow swirled through the streets of New York City, piling higher and higher. In 1872, on December 26, a massive blizzard hit, dumping a whopping 18 inches of snow. It was one of the biggest snowfalls the city had ever seen! Kids probably had a blast sledding and building snowmen, while adults struggled to shovel their way out.
activity_question: How much snow fell during this historic blizzard?
follow_up_question: How do you think people traveled around the city after such a big snowfall?

title: Bright Idea Lights Up a Store
story: Sparks flew in Philadelphia as a store made history! In 1878, on December 26, the first U.S. shop lit up with electric lights. Customers must have been amazed to see the glowing bulbs instead of gas lamps or candles. This bright idea changed shopping forever, making stores safer and more inviting.
activity_question: What type of lighting did the store use for the first time?
follow_up_question: How do you think electric lights changed shopping compared to older lighting methods?
